data.q: What does RTL stand for in web development?
data.e: RTL stands for Right-to-Left, referring to languages and layouts that are read from right to left.

data.q: Which CSS property is used to specify text direction for RTL languages?
data.e: The 'direction' property specifies text direction, with 'rtl' for right-to-left languages.

data.q: Which HTML attribute is used to specify the text direction for an element?
data.e: The 'dir' attribute in HTML sets the text direction, e.g., <div dir="rtl">.

data.q: Why is the use of 'unicode-bidi' CSS property important in RTL layouts?
o: It controls the embedding and override of text direction
It aligns images within a container
It sets the color scheme for RTL
data.e: 'unicode-bidi' controls how embedded bidirectional text is handled, important for mixing LTR and RTL.

data.q: How should web developers handle images in RTL layouts?
o: Use the same as LTR layouts
data.e: Images that imply direction should be flipped horizontally in RTL to maintain correct semantics.

data.q: What role does the 'lang' attribute play in internationalization and RTL support?
o: Specifies the language of the content for screen readers and browsers
Changes the font of the page
data.e: 'lang' helps browsers and assistive technologies understand the language, improving accessibility and rendering.

data.q: Which value of the CSS 'direction' property represents left-to-right text?
data.e: 'ltr' sets left-to-right direction, typically used for languages like English.

data.q: In an RTL web page, where is the horizontal scrollbar typically positioned by default?
data.e: In RTL layouts, scrollbars appear on the left side by default to match the reading direction.

data.q: Which HTML tag attribute automatically adjusts the text direction based on the language?
data.e: Using dir="auto" lets the browser detect and set the appropriate text direction based on content.

data.q: What is a common challenge when designing web forms for RTL languages?
o: Aligning labels and inputs properly from right to left
data.e: Proper alignment of labels and input fields is essential for usability in RTL layouts.
